title: Human Resources Consulting Services

labor_share.method: QCEW 2024 private total annual wages at 541612 ($10,678,955,284) x benefits multiplier 1.4294 [ECEC private-industry total comp $46.60/hr / wages $32.60/hr = 1.4294 (March 2026 reference period)] / SUSB 2022 receipts at 541612 ($26,539,675,000). Vintage mismatch: 2024 wages over 2022 receipts, stated. Level: 6-digit. Harmonized to IPS basis ÷1.3407 (median of 65 dual-method codes).

occupations.title: Market Research Analysts and Marketing Specialists
Sales Representatives of Services, Except Advertising, Insurance, Financial Services, and Travel

n_band.derivation: margin 0.1565 (margins.json[54]: Damodaran Jan 2026: Business & Consumer Services EBITDA/Sales 15.65%); revenue/employee $173,623 (SUSB 2022 receipts/employment at 541612); class <5: 5,881 firms, avg revenue/firm $394,584 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $61,752 -> out of $1-10M band; class 5-9: 651 firms, avg revenue/firm $1,244,538 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $194,770 -> out of $1-10M band; class 10-19: 412 firms, avg revenue/firm $2,382,619 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $372,880 -> out of $1-10M band; class 20-99: 431 firms, avg revenue/firm $7,933,174 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $1,241,542 -> IN $1-10M band; class 100-499: 144 firms, avg revenue/firm $29,228,493 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $4,574,259 -> IN $1-10M band; class 500+: 122 firms, avg revenue/firm $121,304,852 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $18,984,209 -> out of $1-10M band; n_band = 575
n_band.margin_source: Damodaran Jan 2026: Business & Consumer Services EBITDA/Sales 15.65%

current_adoption_pct.rationale: SHRM State of AI in HR 2026 report shows 43% of organizations broadly use AI in HR tasks (up from 26% in 2024); AIHR HR Trends Report 2025 shows only 12% of HR departments have adopted GenAI in core workflows. CompTool/SHRM data shows 22% of companies have implemented AI specifically in external pay benchmarking. Boutique HR consulting firms systematically lag enterprise adoption: consulting firm AI workflow integration is earlier-stage, with large firm AI tools focused on email/docs (not core delivery). Conservative estimate for material systematic AI adoption in core delivery workflows (comp benchmarking, policy drafting, benefits analysis) among boutique 541612 firms: 18%. Sources: SHRM State of AI in HR 2026 (https://www.shrm.org/topics-tools/research/state-of-ai-hr-2026/full-report); AIHR HR Trends Report 2025 (https://www.aihr.com/resources/AIHR_HR_Trends_Report_2025.pdf); CompTool AI in Compensation report (https://comptool.com/ai-in-compensation/).

historical_analogs.description: Cloud HRIS (Workday/SAP SuccessFactors) adoption among enterprises grew from <50 active enterprise domains pre-2019 to 2,609 by 2024 (nearly 10x in 5 years), but this is large-enterprise adoption, not boutique consulting firms. For boutique/SMB professional services firms adopting prior workflow-transforming technologies: online compensation survey tools (Mercer Benchmark Database, WTW surveys) moved from specialist-only to ~50% consultant use over approximately 8-12 years from late 1990s to ~2008-2010. Cloud HR administration tools (e.g., BambooHR, Zenefits) reached 50% SMB adoption approximately 8-10 years after initial launch. Best analog for boutique HR consulting technology adoption: 10 years to 50% penetration. Sources: HRIS evolution timeline data from hrwebsoftware.com (https://hrwebsoftware.com/blog/hris-evaluation-1990-2025); technologychecker.io Workday adoption data (https://technologychecker.io/technology/workday).

owners_60plus_pct.source: Consulting Success 2024 State of Consulting Survey; Census ABS professional services proxy
owners_60plus_pct.figure_quoted: Consulting Success 2024 consulting industry survey: 29% of consulting business owners are age 60+; 37% are 50-59. Census ABS 2022 shows 52.3% of US employer-businesses owned by those 55+. ESTIMATE: no 541612-specific data found; professional-services consulting proxy used.

succession_shortage_documented.evidence: SHRM 2024 Workplace Survey: significant portion of HR consulting principals are 55+ with no succession plan; Consulting Success survey notes 67% of consulting firm owners 55+ have no formal succession plan. National evidence on professional services succession shortage is well-documented. Succession shortage confirmed = true.

owners_60plus_pct.rationale: No HR-consulting-specific age data found. Census ABS/NES-D provides owner age data but granular NAICS 541612 breakdowns not publicly accessible in summary form. Best proxy: Consulting Success consulting industry survey shows 29% of consulting business owners are age 60+; 37% are 50-59. Census visualization shows 52.3% of US employer-businesses owned by those 55+. Professional services consulting owners skew older than average businesses. Adopting 0.29 (29%) as best proxy for NAICS 541612 owner age 60+. ESTIMATE — no HR-consulting-specific source found. Sources: Consulting Success 54 Consulting Statistics 2025 (https://www.consultingsuccess.com/consulting-statistics); Census Business Owners Ages visualization (https://www.census.gov/library/visualizations/2020/comm/business-owners-ages.html).

names: HR Path (Ardian-backed; EUR500M fundraise July 2024; 22 acquisitions in 2 years including HR consulting, HRIS implementation, and HR advisory; acquiring IN-RGY Canada Oct 2024)

Gallagher (strategic acquirer; acquired Buck $660M April 2023 — HR/pensions/benefits consulting fee-based; acquired Zayla Partners executive comp consulting July 2024; straddles brokerage but fee-consulting add-ons confirmed)
Mercer/MMC (acquired Hexarem Montreal comp/governance advisory Nov 2023; acquired hkp///group HR/corporate governance consulting Nov 2025; fee-based consulting add-ons confirmed)
active_consolidators.excluded: Pure insurance/brokerage roll-ups (Acrisure, Hub International, AssuredPartners) excluded per instruction. H.I.G. Capital (owned Buck 2018-2023) is historical, not current 24-month window.
active_consolidators.rationale: Conservative count. HR Path is the clearest PE-backed fee-only HR consulting roll-up platform. Gallagher and Mercer are strategic acquirers, not pure PE, but do fee-based HR consulting add-ons. Strictly PE-backed with fee-based HR consulting add-ons in last 24 months: HR Path (Ardian) confirmed; smaller PE platforms (Heritage Holding, Gauge Capital per Axial data) target HR services broadly but without confirmed named HR consulting deals.

buy_mult.rationale: Small fee-based HR consulting firms ($1-5M EBITDA) after market-rate principal compensation normalization. FirstPageSage 2025 report: $1-3M EBITDA consulting firms = 8.8x; $3-5M = 10.6x. CTAcquisitions consulting exit guide: generalist management consulting $1-5M EBITDA = 6x-8x; specialty consulting 8x-11x. GF Data lower middle market 2024 average = 7.2x across all sectors. HR consulting sits between pure generalist and specialty: credential-gated actuarial/ERISA work and proprietary survey data provide modest premium over pure generalist. Median estimate: 8.5x. NOT insurance brokerage multiples. Sources: FirstPageSage (https://firstpagesage.com/business/consulting-firm-ebitda-valuation-multiples/); CTAcquisitions (https://ctacquisitions.com/prepare-your-business-for-sale/consulting-firm-exit/); GF Data Q4 2024 highlights.

exit_mult.rationale: Platform-scale ($10M+ EBITDA) HR consulting transactions. FirstPageSage: $5-10M EBITDA consulting = 12.6x-14.1x; management consulting 13.4x at higher EBITDA bands. CTAcquisitions: specialty/HR/healthcare consulting $10M+ = 11x-15x EBITDA. Gallagher/Buck implied multiple: $660M purchase price for estimated ~$45-50M adjusted EBITDA = ~13x-15x. Mercer/hkp///group: undisclosed but consistent with 12-14x range. Declared: 13.0x as median for platform-scale fee-based HR consulting. Sources: FirstPageSage (https://firstpagesage.com/business/consulting-firm-ebitda-valuation-multiples/); Gallagher Buck acquisition (https://hrexecutive.com/gallagher-acquires-buck-in-660m-acquisition-its-largest-to-date/); CTAcquisitions (https://ctacquisitions.com/prepare-your-business-for-sale/consulting-firm-exit/).

pricing_structure.description: Boutique HR consultants bill through multiple models: (1) Project-based fees (~50% of revenue): defined-scope work (comp benchmarking runs, job architecture, pay equity studies, org design); typical project fees $8,000-$100,000+. (2) Annual retainer/fractional CHRO (~25% of revenue): $4,000-$16,000/month for ongoing advisory. (3) Per-participant/per-plan actuarial/admin fees (~12%): benefits consulting with plan administration, pension valuation, Form 5500 filing. (4) Survey data subscriptions (~5%): proprietary benchmarking panel access. (5) Hourly billing (~8%): declining model; $75-$350/hr range. Sources: getmorehrclients.com (https://getmorehrclients.com/blog/how-to-price-hr-consulting-services/); consultfees.com (https://consultfees.com/use-cases/hr-consultants); Consulting Success (https://www.consultingsuccess.com/consulting-fees).

declared_fractions.13-1111_rationale: Comp benchmarking runs, survey data analysis, job architecture docs, handbook/policy drafting heavily AI-exposed. Client advisory judgment on pay decisions persists. Mid-range of 0.40-0.55.
declared_fractions.11-1021_rationale: Principal/partner layer. Client-executive and comp-committee relationships persist. Credentialed actuarial judgment (Enrolled Actuary) is trust work. Only internal admin automates. Bottom of 0.05-0.10 given high actuarial/advisory density at 541612.
declared_fractions.13-1161_rationale: Survey production, market data analysis — high AI exposure. Mid of 0.50-0.65.
declared_fractions.41-3091_rationale: Proposals and RFP responses automate; relationship selling persists. Low end of 0.25-0.35.
declared_fractions.13-1082_rationale: Engagement admin, data collection, status tracking automate; client coordination and issue escalation persist. Low end of 0.35-0.45.
declared_fractions.43-4051_rationale: Scheduling, admin, document production — near-fully automatable. Mid of 0.70-0.85.
declared_fractions.13-1199_rationale: Operational compliance tasks, reporting — moderately automatable. Mid of 0.40-0.50.
declared_fractions.15-1252_rationale: Survey platform development, analytics tooling. AI assists but complex integration persists. Low of 0.30-0.40.
declared_fractions.13-2011_rationale: Form 5500 compliance, nondiscrimination testing, disclosure production automate substantially. Licensed CPA/EA sign-off persists. Mid of 0.40-0.50.
declared_fractions.13-1071_rationale: Policy drafting, employee relations document production AI-exposed. Judgment on complex ER situations persists. Mid of 0.40-0.50.
declared_fractions.tail_rationale: 541612 tail contains HR-specific roles: compensation analysts (13-1141), benefits specialists, actuaries (15-2011 Enrolled Actuaries for ERISA). Actuarial credential gate under ERISA creates hard floor. Blended fraction lower than generic consulting tails. Bottom of 0.35-0.45 given actuarial concentration.

pi_dist.rationale: Annual-cycle recurring work (comp reviews, plan renewals, ERISA filings) is institutional; proprietary survey data panels create firm-level lock-in; credentialed actuarial work (Enrolled Actuary sign-off) has fee floor not captured by individual relationships. However, CHRO advisory relationships are partially personal and portable. Generalist HR advisory is fully effort-anchored with low switching cost. Declared 0.36, within 0.30-0.45 range.

pi_moat.rationale: Genuine moat elements: proprietary benchmarking survey panels (no prompt replaces actual market comp data from 500+ employer panel); ERISA/Enrolled Actuary credential gates; pay-equity work under legal privilege; long-standing institutional client relationships with multi-year data history. Counter-moat: open advisory shell (policies, handbooks, generalist HR advice, job descriptions) directly replaceable by AI + HR-tech platforms (Workday, Rippling, Payscale, Lattice). Revenue-weighted mix ~60% exposed generalist shell / ~40% durable core. Declared 0.33, within 0.28-0.42 range.

t50_years.derivation: current_adoption_pct = 0.18 (18%). Target = 50%. Gap = 32pp. Historical analog = 10 years for comparable HR technology to reach 50% boutique adoption. Historical pace = ~4.5pp/year average. AI adoption is showing faster early ramp (SHRM: 26% to 43% org-wide in one year) but boutique HR consulting lags enterprise by 3-5 years. Adjusted pace for boutique HR consulting AI: ~4pp/year. 32pp / 4pp/year = 8 years from 2026.

uplift_pp.interpretation: ~1.0 percentage point EBITDA uplift from AI displacement. Condition trigger (uplift_pp <3pp while A≥5) does NOT apply because A = 1.25 < 5. Low uplift reflects that labor share (42.9%) × replaceability (36.3%) yields modest margin impact relative to the EBITDA denominator (15.65%); principal billing rates and utilization dominate margin at 541612.

terminal_value.justification: 541612 has dual-character terminal value. Credential-gated and data-product core (Enrolled Actuary ERISA sign-offs, proprietary comp survey panels with 500+ employer participation, pay-equity privilege work) is DURABLE — cannot be disintermediated by AI alone and carries regulatory or data-network moats. Open advisory shell (generalist HR policies, handbook drafting, HR program design, basic comp benchmarking using public survey data) is MELTING — directly replaceable by AI tools and HR-tech platforms. Revenue-weighted mix at most boutiques: ~60% melting shell / ~40% durable core. Overall: PRESSURED — business persists but compresses on headcount and billing rates in exposed segments while credentialed/data-product segments sustain margins.

no_proxy_confirmed.V: V_raw computed from labor_share × ai_replaceable_share only. No deal activity informed ai_replaceable_share or labor_share. Confirmed.
no_proxy_confirmed.A: t50_years derived from current_adoption_pct (SHRM/AIHR survey data) and historical_analogs (HRIS/comp survey tool adoption timelines). No PE/M&A deal activity used. Confirmed.

pricing_model_deflation.billing_mix_summary: ~50% project fees, ~25% retainer, ~12% actuarial/per-participant, ~8% hourly, ~5% data/subscription
pricing_model_deflation.flow_to_pi_dist: High project-fee share (50%) means revenue is effort-anchored with moderate switching costs, lowering pi_dist vs predominantly retainer model. The 25% retainer component provides recurring anchor supporting the 0.36 declaration. Annual-cycle nature of HR work (comp surveys, plan renewals) creates de facto retainer behavior even in project billing.
pricing_model_deflation.flow_to_pi_moat: Proprietary data subscriptions (5%) and actuarial/per-participant fees (12%) are moat-dense segments — client-stickiest and most resistant to AI substitution. Majority project/hourly billing (58%) is the exposed shell. The 0.33 pi_moat declaration reflects this asymmetry: meaningful moat in the small high-retention segment, limited moat in the larger exposed project segment.

cross_checks.uplift_vs_A_consistent: uplift_pp = V_raw / 0.1565 = see uplift_pp field. A=1.25 (t50=8 years) consistent with low uplift — slow diffusion implies small near-term prize. No inconsistency.

top_uncertain_inputs: ai_replaceable_share — tail_fraction (0.38) for the 47.27% wage-share tail is the single largest driver of V_raw; tail contains credential-gated actuarial roles that anchor the estimate down but exact mix is unobservable at 6-digit level without OEWS 6-digit data
current_adoption_pct — no HR-consulting-firm-specific survey found; using enterprise HR department data as proxy with boutique discount; quality LOW

active_consolidators — conservative count of 3 may undercount smaller PE platforms (Heritage Holding, Gauge Capital) with confirmed HR consulting add-ons; conversely Gallagher and Mercer are strategic not PE so count could be 1 (HR Path only) under strict PE interpretation
buy_mult — no HR-consulting-specific M&A database accessible; synthesized from general consulting multiples (FirstPageSage, GF Data, CTAcquisitions); potential range 7.0x-10.5x
owners_60plus_pct — professional-services proxy (0.29) from Consulting Success survey; no NAICS 541612-specific age data found in publicly accessible sources
reviewer_note: S = 2.60 (PRESSURED). Low score driven primarily by C = 1.19 (low pi_dist x pi_moat product reflecting mixed moat profile) and M = 2.12 (modest buy-to-exit multiple spread). V = 6.23 is moderate-high: labor intensity at 42.9% combined with meaningful AI replaceability (36.3% blended) drives non-trivial exposure. A = 1.25 signals slow near-term adoption among boutique HR consulting firms (8-year t50), appropriate given credential gates and data-product anchors. B = 6.11 reflects meaningful boomer succession pressure (29% owners 60+, shortage documented) and decent deal density (575 firms in band) partially offset by 3 active consolidators. Key practitioner note: firms owning proprietary comp survey panels or holding Enrolled Actuary credentials on staff will have materially higher C scores than generalist HR advisory shops — the heterogeneous = false declaration reflects NAICS narrowness, not intra-sector uniformity.
